description: §6.6.9 / §19.9.9 (RULING: THE SPLIT) — a CPS-split fn (`@items = ?{…}` is a reactive assignment, so it round-trips) that reads a RAW client `@var` (`@draft`) MARSHALS it into the request body — the value crosses the wire, so no error and NO warning. `add` is the form-submit idiom: the client stub now sends `{ "draft": _scrml_reactive_get("draft") }` and the server reads `_scrml_body["draft"]`. Raw form fields marshal SILENTLY (ordinary form data). Asserts BOTH the E-REACTIVE-003 and W-SERVER-DERIVED-MARSHAL families stay absent. Incidental W-* SQL/program lints ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.6.9: a CPS fn marshals the client-held cells its server batch reads; a raw `@var` form field marshals silently (no trust warning — normal form data).
